rows.definition: The kernel-owned raw attention state: per-position Key and Value tensor rows for the running model, supporting in-place eviction and prefix reuse.
rows.distinction: Local raw tensors the kernel owns - NOT vCache (a control plane over a remote cache) and NOT cachemeta (which owns no tensors).

rows.definition: The virtual API cache: a page-table abstraction that models a remote provider's prefix cache as virtual pages, with a manifest of canonical prefix chains and warmth belief.
rows.distinction: A CONTROL PLANE over a cache you do not own - NOT the KV cache (local tensors) and NOT the provider prompt-cache itself.

rows.definition: The typed metadata contract (tier 1): owns no payloads, names reusable cache entries, and carries their validity / security / residency metadata and typed lookup verdicts.
rows.distinction: The PASSIVE metadata foundation every cache plane builds on - NOT vCache (the active control loop built atop it).

rows.definition: The upstream provider feature: a prefix cached via cache_control breakpoints, reported back as cache_read_input_tokens / cache_creation_input_tokens with a specific pricing multiplier.
rows.distinction: A provider FEATURE you use - NOT vCache (the control loop you build) and NOT the KV cache (local tensors).

rows.definition: A cachemeta entry on plane=provider: the OBSERVED telemetry record of what the provider's prompt cache did (read/creation token counts), marked non-re-serveable local proof.
rows.distinction: The recorded OBSERVATION of the provider feature - NOT the Prompt cache feature itself and NOT a local cache fak can serve from.

rows.definition: The WRITE placement mechanism: the cache_control breakpoint you place to tell the provider where to cache a prefix.
rows.distinction: The breakpoint you PLACE - NOT cache_read (what the provider reports it served) and NOT cache_creation (what it reports it wrote).

rows.definition: The READ telemetry: cache_read_input_tokens, what the provider reports it served from its cache rather than re-prefilling, billed at 0.1x base input.
rows.distinction: What the provider reports it READ - NOT cache_control (the placement) and NOT cache_creation (the write cost).

rows.definition: The WRITE telemetry: cache_creation_input_tokens, what the provider reports it wrote to its cache on this call, billed at 1.25-2.0x base input depending on TTL.
rows.distinction: What the provider reports it WROTE - NOT cache_read (the read rebate) and NOT cache_control (the placement mechanism).

rows.definition: A local token-trie data structure for fast prefix lookup that points INTO KV-cache spans, with materialization binding so cross-model spans are never reused.
rows.distinction: The token-trie INDEX into KV spans - NOT the KV cache (the tensor storage it indexes) and NOT vCache (remote abstraction).

rows.definition: The adapter that translates cachemeta invalidation directives into a remote serving engine's control API (SGLang / vLLM prefix-cache reset or span evict).
rows.distinction: The ADAPTER to a remote engine's cache - NOT cachemeta (the pure contract) and NOT vCache (the policy that triggers it).

rows.definition: Storage of materialized lossy projections (summary, QA, facts) over canonical memory pages, with FAULT / RECOMPUTE / HIT materialization verdicts.
rows.distinction: STORES derived projections - NOT MemoryView (the typed CONTRACT for a projection) and NOT the KV cache (raw tensors).

rows.definition: The typed virtual-view contract binding a lossy derived projection (summary, graph) to its canonical raw-memory source by content digest + byte span, with provenance.
rows.distinction: The CONTRACT binding a projection to its source - NOT ViewCache (the storage of materialized views) and NOT the canonical raw bytes.

rows.definition: The placement POLICY that knows each tier's physical character (HBM / DRAM / NUMA-far / CXL / disk / remote) and demotes hot entries one tier colder under pressure.
rows.distinction: The placement POLICY - NOT the KV cache (the storage it places) and NOT KV transfer (the migration directive it emits).

rows.definition: The migration DIRECTIVE emitted by hardware-aware placement: migrate / offload / restore / route a KV span between tiers.
rows.distinction: The migration DIRECTIVE - NOT the KV cache (the storage) and NOT the hardware-aware policy (which emits it).

rows.definition: The unit of cacheable work in vCache: a cachemeta entry whose identity carries every axis that must match for provider reuse (content digest, model, tokenizer, endpoints, position in prefix DAG).
rows.distinction: A LOGICAL UNIT named by its identity axes - NOT vCache (the control system managing vBlocks) and NOT the KV cache (raw tensors).
gaps: not yet written into the concept glossary - currently defined-only

rows.definition: The interface abstracting the per-position bytes a given attention variant caches and how to reconstruct per-head K/V from those bytes (standard vs MLA).
rows.distinction: The reconstruction INTERFACE over cached bytes - NOT the KV cache (the storage) and NOT MLAConfig (the projection geometry).
gaps: not yet written into the concept glossary - currently defined-only

rows.definition: The low-rank MLA projection geometry (DeepSeek V2/V3): latent width, RoPE dim, and down/up projection matrices for compressing the K/V cache.
rows.distinction: The projection PARAMETERS - NOT KVLayout (the interface that uses them) and NOT the KV cache (the cached tensors).
gaps: not yet written into the concept glossary - currently defined-only
